purpose: Metadata layer over the original deduped_question_bank.json. The bank file is NEVER modified. This file maps bank question IDs to current-name notes, tested concepts, clue words, exam traps, and confidence so the app can surface annotations alongside bank questions.

schema.topic: Official sub-objective (matches chapter_content.json keys, e.g. '2.2 Building the pipelines').
schema.tested_concept: Short label of what the question is really testing.
schema.clue_words: List of phrases that signal the correct answer.
schema.current_product_name_note: Optional. If the question or options use an old product name, current name and note.
schema.outdated_wording_note: Optional. If wording is stale (e.g. flat-rate, Stackdriver, Data Catalog standalone) but the underlying answer is still valid.
schema.exam_trap: Optional. The wrong-answer pattern this question rewards spotting.
schema.confidence: high | medium | low — how sure the annotation is. Low = ambiguous question, may have multiple defensible answers.

coverage_note: Starter set covering all 19 official sub-objectives. Adding annotations to every bank question is a follow-up content task; the schema is fixed so future entries append cleanly.

annotations.tested_concept: Authorized views / sharing aggregate without source access
annotations.exam_trap: dataViewer on the source table is the wrong over-share answer.

annotations.tested_concept: Data residency / regional storage for compliance
annotations.current_product_name_note: Use Org Policy 'resourcemanager.allowedLocations' to enforce; Assured Workloads packages this for regulated industries.
annotations.exam_trap: Multi-region (US, EU) crosses country boundaries — not always residency-compliant.

annotations.tested_concept: NoSQL choice for telemetry / time-series at scale
annotations.exam_trap: Picking BigQuery (analytical, not sub-10 ms point reads) or Firestore (document, not scale of telemetry).

annotations.tested_concept: ACID + minimal ops — Spanner vs Cloud SQL
annotations.exam_trap: Choosing Cloud SQL when global ACID and horizontal scale are required.
gsutil/gcloud storage when bandwidth makes online transfer infeasible — Transfer Appliance is the right call past ~20 TB on slow links.
Choosing a synchronous HTTP queue or Cloud Tasks when Pub/Sub is the canonical decoupling answer.

annotations.tested_concept: BigQuery Data Transfer Service for SaaS sources
annotations.exam_trap: Building a custom pipeline when BigQuery Data Transfer Service has a managed connector.
Underestimating bandwidth time — Transfer Appliance often beats online for very large one-time loads.

annotations.tested_concept: Pipeline design for variable data quality
annotations.exam_trap: Loading directly into BQ without a quality gate; the answer should land raw, then validate, then load.
Choosing batch when sub-minute is the requirement.

annotations.tested_concept: Secure automated pipeline with service accounts
annotations.exam_trap: User credentials for production pipeline runs — always SA + Workload Identity.

annotations.current_product_name_note: If the answer references Pub/Sub Lite for Kafka migrations, current Google guidance prefers Managed Service for Apache Kafka.
annotations.exam_trap: Direct DB writes from many servers — buffer through Pub/Sub.
Cloud Scheduler when there are multi-step dependencies — Composer is the right tier.
Self-managing Airflow on Compute Engine when Composer is available.

annotations.tested_concept: Storage choice for relational tables at TB scale
annotations.exam_trap: BigQuery for OLTP; Bigtable when JOINs are required.

annotations.tested_concept: Object storage for very large files
annotations.exam_trap: Standard class for cold data — lifecycle to Coldline/Archive.

annotations.topic: 3.2 Planning for using a data warehouse
annotations.exam_trap: Custom REST clients when JDBC/ODBC drivers and BigQuery API exist.

annotations.topic: 3.2 Planning for using a data warehouse
annotations.outdated_wording_note: If the question references 'flat-rate slots', note that flat-rate purchasing was retired 2023-07-05 — replaced by BigQuery Editions.
annotations.exam_trap: Picking on-demand for predictable heavy workload — Editions is the cost-cap answer.
Forgetting class-minimum durations (Nearline 30d, Coldline 90d, Archive 365d) — early deletion incurs a charge.
Persistent cluster with embedded metastore vs ephemeral cluster + Dataproc Metastore (Hive). Ephemeral with external metastore is canonical.

annotations.current_product_name_note: Use Dataplex Universal Catalog (rebranded Knowledge Catalog April 2026); standalone Data Catalog discontinued 2026-01-30.
annotations.exam_trap: Centralizing all data into one BQ project when a federated mesh fits — Dataplex enforces cross-domain policy.
Choosing batch refresh when BI Engine + materialized view delivers sub-second freshness.

annotations.topic: 4.2 Preparing data for AI and ML
annotations.tested_concept: Pretrained API vs custom model — simplest capable path
annotations.current_product_name_note: Vertex AI is now under Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (Cloud Next 2026 rebrand); aiplatform SDK names unchanged.
annotations.exam_trap: Custom training when a pretrained API (Vision / NLP / Speech / Translation / Document AI) handles the task.

annotations.topic: 4.2 Preparing data for AI and ML
annotations.exam_trap: More epochs as a cure for overfitting — actually makes it worse.

annotations.tested_concept: BigQuery dataset sharing — IAM vs authorized view
annotations.exam_trap: Granting dataViewer on the source when an authorized view / authorized dataset is the right fit.

annotations.tested_concept: BigQuery cost optimization for scheduled loads
annotations.outdated_wording_note: Old wording may say 'flat-rate slots' — read as BigQuery Editions today.
annotations.exam_trap: Loading via streaming inserts when daily batch loads are free.

annotations.current_product_name_note: BigQuery Editions replaced flat-rate (2023-07-05). Use Editions + reservations + assignments for workload isolation.
annotations.exam_trap: On-demand for predictable heavy use; one shared reservation for ETL + BI.
Confusing cluster autoscaling with job concurrency — they are separate controls.

annotations.tested_concept: Container deployment for data processing on GKE
annotations.exam_trap: Picking Cloud Run when stateful Spark / batch needs a Kubernetes scheduler.

annotations.topic: 5.3 Organizing workloads based on business requirements
annotations.current_product_name_note: Dialogflow CX / Vertex AI Agent Builder (now Agent Studio under Gemini Enterprise) is the modern chatbot answer.
annotations.exam_trap: Building from scratch when a managed conversational AI service exists.

annotations.topic: 5.3 Organizing workloads based on business requirements
annotations.current_product_name_note: Use BigQuery Editions with reservations and assignments to guarantee baselines per workload.
annotations.exam_trap: Increasing on-demand quota when a reservation is the architectural answer.

annotations.current_product_name_note: Stackdriver Logging is now Cloud Logging.
annotations.exam_trap: Forgetting Data Access logs are off by default for most services — must be explicitly enabled.
Adding nodes blindly when Key Visualizer would identify the row-key hotspot.

annotations.topic: 5.5 Maintaining awareness of failures and mitigating impact
annotations.exam_trap: Relying on backup tools when object versioning / soft-delete is built-in.

annotations.topic: 5.5 Maintaining awareness of failures and mitigating impact
annotations.exam_trap: Single-region BQ dataset with no snapshot/export when cross-region recovery is required.
